title: Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple
tagline: Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Sri Veera Venkata Satyanarayana Swamy Temple is a Hindu-Vaishnavite temple located in Annavaram in Kakinada district of Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Sri Veera Venkata Satyanarayana Swamy Temple is a Hindu-Vaishnavite temple located in Annavaram in Kakinada district of Andhra Pradesh, India. Dedicated to Lord Satyanarayana Swamy, an incarnation of Lord Vishnu, the temple is situated on Ratnagiri Hill. It is one of the most visited religious sites in Andhra Pradesh and is recognized as one of the state's wealthiest temples. The temple holds significant importance as a major pilgrimage centre and is regarded by many as the second most prominent in the state after the Tirumala Venkateswara Temple.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajahmundry Airport (65 km) and the nearest railway station is Visakhapatnam Junction (107 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Annavaram Satyanarayana Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sankhavaram, Billavaka, Tetagunta, Parupaka, Gidajam.
